events.title: WHO and Brazil urge world leaders to finalise Pandemic Agreement to prevent future global health crises
events.description: Global officials are calling on world leaders to finalise a crucial international agreement aimed at preventing future pandemics, according to a joint letter issued on Monday.

events.title: Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk
events.description: Abdiqadir Salah was pierced by shrapnel in a bombing that killed 12 in Somalia. But as the US denies civilians were hurt they face no hope of compensation Read more: Killed walking home from school: why did Somali children become targets of US drone strikes? A seven-year-old boy who was riddled with shrapnel during a deadly US airstrike in Somalia faces losing his ability to walk unless he has a £750 emergency operation. But Abdiqadir Salah’s family cannot afford the surgery and the US – which refuses to admit that any civilians were killed or injured during its attack six months ago – appears unwilling to pay compensation to those affected by airstrikes in Somalia. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk

events.title: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
events.description: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts

events.title: Ukraine: Latest Russian attack kills civilians, damages cultural landmark
events.description: Several civilians were killed and dozens more were injured in the latest wave of overnight attacks in Ukraine that targeted the capital Kyiv, the city of Kharkiv and the country’s history and cultural heritage, the United Nations said on Monday.

events.title: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
events.description: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children

events.title: Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children
events.description: Drought, extreme heat and heatwaves are the most prevalent trio of hazards endangering millions of children globally, warned a newly released climate report by the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children

events.title: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
events.description: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

events.title: Christian leaders hold emergency summit in Jerusalem to confront global rise in antisemitism
events.description: International Christian Embassy Jerusalem is hosting an emergency summit addressing rising antisemitism and replacement theology amid church leaders' silence.

events.title: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
events.description: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation

events.title: DC Guard shooting suspect stares down death penalty in first court appearance
events.description: Federal prosecutors are seeking the death penalty against Rahmanullah Lakanwal, who pleaded not guilty in killing National Guard Specialist Sarah Beckstrom in Washington, D.C.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 DC Guard shooting suspect stares down death penalty in first court appearance

events.title: Polish civilians take army training course amid spectre of war with Russia
events.description: Poland, which is regularly targeted by Russian hybrid attacks, takes the threat of war with Moscow very seriously. The country now spends nearly 5 percent of its GDP on defence, and aims to train its civilians too, so they will know how to react if war breaks out. To this end, it has introduced a programme of one-day training sessions with the army, entitled "Always Ready", which have turned out to be a runaway success. FRANCE 24's Adrien Sarlat and Jan Garstecki joined some of the participants at a session near Warsaw.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Polish civilians take army training course amid spectre of war with Russia

events.title: US signals to return of sanctions on Russia as G7 unites around Ukraine
events.description: G7 leaders agreed on June 16 to intensify pressure on Russia to end more than four years of war against Ukraine, with US President Donald Trump saying Moscow should "make a deal" and indicating Washington could re-impose waived sanctions. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky joined the summit at the French resort of Evian, hoping that with a deal between the US and Iran ostensibly in the works, the Trump administration will now turn its attention to solving Europe's largest armed conflict since World War II.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US signals to return of sanctions on Russia as G7 unites around Ukraine

events.title: Thousands flock back to south Lebanon despite Israeli strikes amid US-Iran deal
events.description: Lebanon has been targeted by fresh Israeli strikes despite the peace deal agreement announced by the US and Israel. Despite the attacks and warnings from their government, many south Lebanese are desperate to go back home, with thousands of people driving back. But civilians still remain sceptic. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis tells us more from Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Thousands flock back to south Lebanon despite Israeli strikes amid US-Iran deal

events.title: What we know about Russian warship that fired warning shots in English Channel
events.description: A Russia warship fired warning shots at a UK-registered yacht in the English Channel in an incident that has intensified national security concerns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What we know about Russian warship that fired warning shots in English Channel

events.title: UN urges adherence to mine ban treaty
events.description: Countries must uphold international law limiting the use of anti-personnel mines, which kill and maim civilians long after conflicts have ended, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Tuesday.

events.title: Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears
events.description: The Security Council on Friday weighed the future of the UN mechanism responsible for completing the remaining work of the international tribunals for Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia, as members debated how to preserve its legacy while bringing its mandate to an orderly close.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears

events.title: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
events.description: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emergency.

events.title: Two children dead after pram hit by car in Sydney despite ‘phenomenal’ bystander efforts to save them
events.description: Police found two children – both aged under five – had been struck by a car in Cabramatta, in the city’s south-west Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two children in a pram have been struck and killed by a car in Sydney’s south-west. Emergency services were called to the intersection of Joseph and Gilbert streets, Cabramatta at about 3.15pm on Wednesday after reports of a crash, New South Wales police said. Continue reading...

events.title: 'First line of defence' has collapsed in biggest ever outbreak of rare Ebola strain
events.description: The number of Ebola infections in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is likely far higher than official figures, Oxfam has warned, due to lack of clean water and sanitation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'First line of defence' has collapsed in biggest ever outbreak of rare Ebola strain

events.title: Suspect in custody after Delaware hospital shooting leaves at least 1 person dead, another wounded
events.description: Two people were shot at Wilmington Hospital in Delaware on Tuesday that left one of the victims dead. A suspect has been taken into custody.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Suspect in custody after Delaware hospital shooting leaves at least 1 person dead, another wounded

events.title: India: Why a country of 1.4 billion is not in the football World Cup
events.description: The world's most populous country is still missing from football's biggest stage.

events.title: Morocco: The 2030 World Cup is more than sports, it is a national project
events.description: Hosting the 2030 World Cup is more than just a sporting event for Rabat. While the kingdom invests heavily in infrastructure and prestige projects, doubts and social conflicts grow alongside a sense of pride and hope.

events.title: World News in Brief: Risky return home in Lebanon, displacement in Gaza, emergency funding for Somalia
events.description: Despite the agreement announced on Sunday between the United States and Iran, displaced people in Lebanon continue to face difficulties in returning to their communities, the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) has reported.

events.title: AFP investigates banner stunt during Pauline Hanson’s National Press Club speech
events.description: Activist group GetUp claims responsibility for incident, saying ‘we thought the occasion deserved some honesty’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian federal police is investigating a protest incident during Pauline Hanson’s speech at the National Press Club after political activist group GetUp claimed responsibility for the stunt. In a statement on Wednesday afternoon, the National Press Club said it had referred the incident to police after concluding none of its own staff were involved. Continue reading...

events.title: World News in Brief: Reduced violence in Lebanon, shortages in Gaza, rising debt impacts development funding
events.description: Although United Nations peacekeepers continue to observe violence and exchanges of fire in Lebanon, the level is significantly reduced when compared to the weekend, UN Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Tuesday.

events.title: Resettlement remains out of reach for millions of refugees
events.description: As millions of people continue to flee their countries due to war or persecution, UNHCR, the UN refugee agency, called on Tuesday for an urgent expansion of refugee resettlement programmes, warning that available places continue to fall short of global needs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Resettlement remains out of reach for millions of refugees

events.title: Israeli air strikes on Lebanon continue despite US-Iran deal
events.description: There has been a reduction in violence since the US-Iran agreement was announced, but attacks have not stopped.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli air strikes on Lebanon continue despite US-Iran deal

events.title: U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 1, leaves 2 survivors
events.description: The U.S. military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, killing one man and leaving two survivors. This brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes to at least 208.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 1, leaves 2 survivors

events.title: Foiled White House attack: Group planned to use snipers and drones, says FBI
events.description: The FBI has said it thwarted a planned attack on the UFC mixed martial arts event on the White House lawn on the weekend, with the attackers allegedly planning to use explosive-laden drones to strike the north side of the White House while snipers would shoot people as they fled. Five people have been charged over the plot and they were allegedly motivated in part by anger at the handling of investigative files related to sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Foiled White House attack: Group planned to use snipers and drones, says FBI

events.title: DR Congo Ebola outbreak could become worst in history as thousands not being traced
events.description: Healthcare workers and officials are continuing to sound the alarm over the surging Ebola crisis. On June 16, the head of Africa's Centre for Disease Control and Prevention said that tens of thousands of people who have been in contact with ill patients in the Democratic Republic of Congo are currently not being traced and that the outbreak could become the worst in history - surpassing the 2014-2016 outbreak in Guinea, Liberia and Sierra Leone that killed over 11,000 people.

events.title: Nancy Guthrie mystery exposes new threat targeting unsuspecting Americans letting down their guards
events.description: Experts warn criminals use social media and open source information to find wealthy targets as wrench attacks surge 41% in early 2026, per CertiK data.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Nancy Guthrie mystery exposes new threat targeting unsuspecting Americans letting down their guards

events.title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
events.description: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel

events.title: Toronto police link dozens of shootings to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network
events.description: Young adults and teens are being recruited through apps like Telegram and paid to carry out attacks, officials say Police investigators in Toronto have said that dozens of shootings – including one at the US consulate in March – are linked to a “multilayered” gun-for-hire network that is also responsible for attacks on synagogues around Canada’s largest city. Toronto’s police chief, Myron Demkiw, told reporters on Tuesday that young adults and teenagers are being recruited through encrypted messaging apps such as Signal, Telegram and WhatsApp by “bad actors” and paid by the networks to carry out the attacks. Shooters are required to film their attacks in order to get paid. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Toronto police link dozens of shootings to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network

events.title: UK investigating warning shots fired by Russian ship at yacht
events.description: Russia appears to have fired warning shots at a UK-registered yacht in the English Channel on June 16. British authorities say they are investigating the incident but so far treating it as isolated. The Grigorovich had been missioned with escorting tankers carrying Russian oil through the channel in the past. There were no reports of injuries or damage to the yacht.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK investigating warning shots fired by Russian ship at yacht

events.title: Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict
events.description: UN Secretary General António Guterres welcomed on Sunday a new peace deal between the United States and Iran, calling it a “critical step” toward ending the conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict

events.title: South Carolina personal trainer goes missing, last spotted walking toward wooded area
events.description: Lexington police say Elena Moore's disappearance is very out of character after she was last seen leaving a gym and heading toward nearby woods.

events.title: Pauline Hanson makes clear what a One Nation government would be like – it’s an ugly picture
events.description: The One Nation leader offered a series of inflammatory statements at the National Press Club that may play well with her supporters but will alarm many others Pauline Hanson says Australia ‘must be monocultural’ in National Press Club speech Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast For months, voters have been telling anyone who’ll listen that they want Pauline Hanson. Pollsters , journalists and the (current) political establishment have all heard how the major parties are letting down the country and One Nation is the answer. Continue reading...

events.title: India is once again not participating in the World Cup. Will it ever?

Finally, we ask why India is not in the World Cup and discover Brooklyn Beckham's latest family feud.

events.title: Woman says she ‘didn’t understand mental health’ before daughter experiencing acute psychosis killed another child
events.description: Girl told mother she thought about killing people ‘all the time’, inquest into 10-year-old Biddy Porter’s death hears Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast As a teenager descended into psychosis, killing animals, having visions and hearing voices, her mother consulted a naturopath believing the girl’s behaviour was due to a hormonal imbalance, an inquest has been told. Two weeks after the 14-year-old confessed to her mother that she thought about killing people “all the time”, the girl killed 10-year-old Biddy Porter at a property in rural New South Wales. Continue reading...

events.title: UN chief visits Haiti, where a new 'gang-suppression force' will be deployed
events.description: U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres's visit to Port-au-Prince comes as gang violence persists. According to U.N. data, 2,300 people have been killed in Haiti this year, with another 100 kidnapped.

events.title: 5 charged in plot to attack Trump's UFC event, DOJ says

events.title: Fatal shooting of Russian anti-Putin artist likely politically motivated, Polish PM says
events.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said Wednesday that the fatal shooting of exiled Russian artist Semyon Skrepetsky was likely a political assassination. The Kremlin critic, known for provocative caricatures of President Vladimir Putin and other political figures, was shot dead by an unidentified gunman in eastern Poland on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Fatal shooting of Russian anti-Putin artist likely politically motivated, Polish PM says

events.title: Global fish production hits new high, but climate threats loom
events.description: Aquaculture now supplies most of the fish people eat, a landmark report by the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) reveals, but rising temperatures and overfishing are putting the future of the industry at risk.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: 'End of an Era': Facing an increasingly adversarial US, Europe embraces proactive defence strategy
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome François Heisbourg, Chair of the International Institute for Strategic Studies. He argues that the apparent Iran-related "peace deal" announced by Donald Trump remains opaque, incomplete, and potentially unstable, with crucial details unresolved.

events.title: FRANCE 24 Exclusive: what Strait of Hormuz locals think of the Iran-US deal
events.description: Since the US-Israel strikes on Iran began on February 28th the Strait of Hormuz has effectively been blocked - a key strategy by Iran that upended global oil supplies and pressured its opponents. But what has it meant for the seafarers stranded there? Or those who call its islands home? With plans for the key shipping channel to be fully reopened after Friday June 19, FRANCE 24's correspondent Reza Sayah headed out onto the water to talk with people in the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 FRANCE 24 Exclusive: what Strait of Hormuz locals think of the Iran-US deal

events.description: Animal welfare groups say the bust highlights the 'enormous scale' of the country's cat meat trade.

events.title: South African jazz pianist Abdullah Ibrahim dies aged 91
events.description: The accomplished musician, who recorded over 70 albums in his career, died peacefully in Germany after a short illness The South African jazz composer and pianist Abdullah Ibrahim has died at the age of 91. His family announced his death in a statement released on Monday. Continue reading...

events.title: Bank of Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high … of 1%
events.description: Country acts amid Iran war inflation pressures, but US Fed and Bank of England expected to hold rates Business live – latest updates The Bank of Japan (BoJ) has raised interest rates to a 31-year high as it tries to dampen inflationary pressures created by the Iran war. Policymakers in Tokyo raised the BoJ’s short-term policy rate by a quarter of one percentage point, to 1% from 0.75% , and warned that companies were passing on rising oil costs to each other at a “relatively fast pace”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bank of Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high … of 1%

events.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
events.description: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring

events.title: Ghana to advance reparatory justice at first major gathering since landmark UN resolution
events.description: Heads of state and participants from more than 80 countries at three-day event in Accra to pursue actionable commitments to reconciliation and restitution Ghana is hosting a conference to advance the continent’s push for reparatory justice after the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Heads of state and government, ministers, civil society representatives, historians, researchers and legal experts representing more than 80 countries are converging in the capital, Accra, for the three-day event, billed Next Steps , which starts on Wednesday. It is the first major gathering on the issue since the resolution was adopted . Continue reading...

events.title: From camel coats to guochao: Max Mara woos China’s luxury brand consumers
events.description: Fashion house pays tribute to Chinese style with its 75th anniversary catwalk show in Shanghai “New York may be the city that never sleeps, but Shanghai doesn’t even sit down.” For the British designer Ian Griffiths, who encountered this line in the New Yorker, it summed up why China’s biggest city was the right place to celebrate Max Mara’s 75th anniversary. “Max Mara is a product for metropolitan women, and it would be patronising to assume that a metropolitan wardrobe should be western-centric,” Griffiths said. events.title: Messi becomes joint World Cup all-time scorer with hat trick against Algeria
events.description: Argentina beat Algeria 3-0 on June 16. Lionel Messi's hat trick of goals came 20 years to the day after his World Cup debut. Messi is now the first man to play in six World Cups and the tournament's joint all-time scorer, alongside Germany's Miroslav Klose, with 16 goals. The player was praised by supporters on both sides.

Viktoriia Honcharuk was just starting her career when the war broke out. But she felt that if she had children in the future she wanted to be able to look them in the eyes and say she did everything possible to make sure they had a future. She spoke to us in Perspective.

events.title: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies
events.description: Outpouring of public grief for Lidia ‘Taty’ Almeida, leader of group of mothers that has marched every week since 1977 The human rights activist Lidia “Taty” Almeida – who spent more than half a century searching for her son after he was forcibly disappeared by Argentina’s military junta – has died aged 95, prompting a public outpouring of grief. Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched around the square outside Argentina’s presidential palace every Thursday since 1977 , demanding the return of children who were disappeared during the country’s 1976-1983 dictatorship. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies

events.title: Press club refers banner incident to federal police – as it happened

The shock jock agreed to drop his $85m lawsuit against KIIS FM, in return for a cash payment and $1.5m in advertising contra on ARN’s stations for a new broadcasting venture yet to be announced. The inquiry heard evidence of residents being pressured to relocate, deceived about their rights and fearful of losing their homes and communities. Those allegations are serious, but Labor’s response shows they haven’t taken them seriously … They don’t care what the experts say or what people think. events.title: Brazil Supreme Court sentences Bolsonaro's son over US lobbying

The third son of former far-right President Jair Bolsonaro was found guilty of pressuring US officials to impose economic sanctions on his own country. It was a bid to influence his father’s Supreme Court trial — with the elder Bolsonaro currently serving a 27-year sentence for a failed coup attempt in 2022.

events.title: Woman, 21, dies after being thrown from Brazil rope jump bridge without harness
events.description: Instructors hurled Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas into 40-metre abyss without attaching safety equipment A 21-year-old woman who died when two rope-jumping instructors threw her from a bridge without first harnessing her to security equipment has been buried in Brazil ’s São Paulo state. Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas was rope jumping on Saturday at Ponte do Esqueleto, an abandoned bridge in the municipality of Limeira where tourists practise extreme sports. The young woman, who aspired to become a physical education teacher, had asked to be launched from the bridge airplane-style, with two instructors hoisting her above their shoulders as she spread out her arms. events.title: Ahead of Trump's dinner at Versailles, a look at its previous guests over the decades
events.description: The three-day G7 summit is wrapping up in France on Wednesday, but for US President Donald Trump and French President Emmanual Macron there's still something on the menu: dinner at the Palace of Versailles. For more than 400 years, the opulent residence has welcomed many foreign dignitaries and heads of state. This time round, it appears Paris is focused on showcasing its ties with Washington, rather than fixating on their recent barbs.

events.title: France looks to exchange its 'support to Iran deal with increased US support to Ukraine'
events.description: France is launching a charm operation on the US. Macron is hoping to get increased support to Ukraine and bring back US sanctions on Russia and Putin, with France hoping to seal the deal during a dinner in the sumptuous palace of Versailles on June 17. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us more about what France is trying to achieve with the United States from Evian.

events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

The institution’s principal has done everything he can to prevent the spread of the virus: installing hand-washing basins at the entrance, providing alcohol-based hand rub for parents, making pupils bring packed lunches instead of eating in the canteen, and banning food sellers from outside the gates. events.title: G7 vows new Russia sanctions amid optimism for Ukraine peace
events.description: G7 leaders, such as German Chancellor Merz, are sounding more upbeat about the push for peace in Ukraine. The G7 is looking to hit Russia with new energy sanctions as the Strait of Hormuz is expected to reopen.

events.title: Iconic Paris canal opens to swimmers as residents seek ways to cope with heatwave
events.description: As France is bracing for another heatwave, Paris is allowing swimming in one of its canals so residents could cope with the heat. It will be the second spell of hot temperatures this year, after an unusually scorching week in May. Later in the summer, swimmers will also be able to cool off at designated points along the Seine River. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell tells us more from the canal.

events.title: After a long wait, JR's Pont Neuf installation opens to the public
events.description: The Pont Neuf Cavern, an artwork by French street artist JR, sometimes referred to as the French Banksy, is now open to visitors in Paris. The installation made from printed fabric and air transforms Paris' oldest bridge into an artificial cavern, through a visual, auditory and olfactory experience. A sound installation by Thomas Bangalter, formerly of the French electronic duo Daft Punk, accompanies the work, filling the cavern with low rumbles, echoes and pulses.

events.title: Paris's BHV ends short but controversial partnership with Shein
events.description: It was a partnership beset by controversy, and now it's over. Department store BHV is breaking up with Chinese-founded retailer Shein, after new management came in to take over the beleaguered Parisian icon, announcing the end of the contract between the two and admitting that a strategic error had been made. However, seven other BHVs in the Paris region will retain a partnership.

events.description: Convicted killer Karmelo Anthony claims he is penniless while seeking a taxpayer-funded attorney, but records show his parents formed a Texas business.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Karmelo Anthony claims he's penniless as business records tied to parents draw scrutiny

events.title: Luigi Mangione supporter working for far-left DC mayoral candidate likened to Mamdani
events.description: Makia Green, political director for D.C. mayoral candidate Janeese Lewis George, called for accused assassin Luigi Mangione to be freed and pardoned.

events.title: Teen accused of killing stepsister on Carnival cruise ship ordered detained before murder trial
events.description: Teen accused of killing stepsister Anna Kepner on Carnival cruise ship ordered detained after judge finds no release conditions can protect community.

events.title: 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope
events.description: A 21-year-old woman died after instructors launched her from a bridge without attaching safety ropes during a rope-jumping event near São Paulo, Brazil.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope

events.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
events.description: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.

events.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
events.description: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
events.description: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.
